items.title: Mining set to restart at one of the NT's biggest gold reserves

children.content: After being dormant for nearly 30 years, the Mount Todd gold mine near Katherine is gearing up to restart production. While there are calls for local Aboriginal people to have the "first crack" at mining jobs, the operator is planning to engage a largely FIFO workforce.

gallery.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

thumbnail.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: First Indigenous SANFL player David Kantilla inducted into hall of fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A shirtless man in jeans with his face blurred is surrounded by three police officers who hold his arms

items.title: Miner Nathan River Resources collapses owing at least $300m

children.content: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

image.altText: A haul truck is being loaded with iron ore in the open-pit of Roper Bar mine in the NT.

items.title: Police charge trio with Darwin man's murder in alleged 'vicious attack'

children.content: Police have charged three men who allegedly beat 48-year-old Darwin man Martin Wiberg to death in a "vicious attack" at the Casuarina bus stop earlier this year with his murder.

image.altText: Man holds three kittens under his chin

items.title: NT youth mental health inpatient facility lacking basic safety standards

children.content: The Northern Territory's only youth mental health inpatient ward failed to meet 16 national safety standards, documents obtained under Freedom of Information laws have revealed.

gallery.altText: a multi-storey hospital building with a sign saying pandemic clinic visible

thumbnail.altText: a multi-storey hospital building with a sign saying pandemic clinic visible

items.title: Leaders of flood ravaged community say locals will return to no services

children.content: Three months ago the town of Nauiyu / Daly River was submerged in the region's worst ever floods. Now, Indigenous leaders worry residents are returning to a community with no services, if the NT and federal government do not step in.

image.altText: Three women and a man pose for the camera.

items.title: Arnhem Land culture takes centre stage at Barunga Festival 2026

children.content: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

image.altText: A traditional Aboriginal dancer.

items.title: Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld

children.content: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

image.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

items.title: Kings and Queens of the Desert crowned as outback Finke race ends

children.content: Returning legends of the Finke Desert Race, David Walsh and Travis Robinson, have been crowned Kings of the Desert at the gruelling two-day off-road race, with Walsh winning on bikes for a record-equalling sixth time and Robinson claiming victory on cars.

image.altText: A small group of people in car racing outfits standing together on a stage and smiling, with one holding a winner's shield high.

items.title: Protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack, homes, cars and bus set on fire

children.content: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

image.altText: Car torched in street.

gallery.altText: Four people stand watching a bus on fire, in a Belfast high street. A fire crew attends.

thumbnail.altText: Four people stand watching a bus on fire, in a Belfast high street. A fire crew attends.

items.title: Judge throws out appeals over homophobic tweet judgement

children.content: A Federal Court judge has dismissed an appeal against a judgement which ordered New South Wales MP Mark Latham to pay $140,000 to independent MP Alex Greenwich over a homophobic tweet in 2023.

image.altText: Mark Latham wearing a suit speaking to media on the street outside court.

items.title: 'We didn't know how dangerous he was': How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

image.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: Why Xi Jinping made an exception to see Kim Jong Un in person

children.content: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

gallery.altText: Two men wearing suits seen sitting and smiling in the crowd at a stadium.
Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un walk together along a decorated red carpet.
Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un sit down to a banquet.

thumbnail.altText: Two men wearing suits seen sitting and smiling in the crowd at a stadium.
Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un walk together along a decorated red carpet.
Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un sit down to a banquet.

items.title: Thirteen government agencies embroiled in potential data breach

children.content: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

image.altText: A stack of documents sitting in an office, generic.

items.title: Musk's xAI hit with class action over power plant's 'inescapable' noise

children.content: US residents say a plant powering data centres has caused them emotional distress and reduced the value of their properties.

image.altText: a fenced industiral site - metal pies and tubes criss cross in front of a warehouse

items.title: 'It breaks us to have to live like this': Overcrowding in outback NSW

children.content: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

gallery.altText: A woman standing in a kitchen holding a baby.

thumbnail.altText: A woman standing in a kitchen holding a baby.

items.title: How police may have inadvertently saved this man from Ivan Milat

children.content: Etched in Steven Clark's memory are the protruding teeth and moustache of the driver he says picked him up as a teenager — a man he now believes was serial killer Ivan Milat.

image.altText: Man wearing a hat stands on the side of a road.

items.title: Shop worker 'concerned' by security response after knife comment

children.content: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

image.altText: Shivanya in grey top holding bag outside shopping centre

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

gallery.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

thumbnail.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

avatar.alt: A young woman with blonde hair and glasses

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: Quiz: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off to see the world

children.content: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

image.altText: Chop sticks hover over a plat of thinly sliced raw white fish with a green garnish.

items.title: Is Australia sucking the joy out of childhood?

children.content: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: playtime.

gallery.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass
Two children grasp an overhead rope as they balance on slipper, mud-covered concrete tubes between two gum trees.

thumbnail.altText: Two young white kids holding hands, running through long grass
Two children grasp an overhead rope as they balance on slipper, mud-covered concrete tubes between two gum trees.

items.title: Court rejects appeal to increase sentence of killer driver

children.content: The Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal to increase the jail term of Christopher Dillon Joannidis, who killed five people in a horrific 2023 crash in Northern Victoria.

image.altText: Christopher Dillon Joannidis holding his partner Eleanor Theebom's hand walking across a road

items.title: 41yo charged with murder over Wollongong stabbing

children.content: Police have charged a man and say they expect more arrests in relation to the stabbing of Brandon Yates, who was found fatally injured in an apartment in Wollongong in 2025.

image.altText: Black and white photo of shirtless man with backward cap

items.title: Two people killed in truck, car crash south of Nowra

children.content: The Princes Highway remains closed in both directions as police investigate the cause of the crash.

image.altText: Long line of cars on highway

items.title: Man found dead in Melbourne park had suffered burns, police say

children.content: Police say the man appeared to have suffered burns and was discovered by a passer-by in parkland in Melbourne's east.

image.altText: A police vehicle.

items.title: 'Just an absolute tip': Residents frustrated with rise of illegal dumping

children.content: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

gallery.altText: A truck in a parking bay, with massive amounts of household waste piled up along the fenceline.
Rubbish and household items piled against a brick wall, in a carpark.
large bags filled with household rubbish.

large bags filled with household rubbish.

items.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church

children.content: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

image.altText: Colourful ribbons on a church ground fence

items.title: Further $105m federal-state bailout announced for Nyrstar smelters

children.content: The federal, Tasmanian and South Australian governments have contributed to a new funding package for Nyrstar's Hobart and Port Pirie smelters, including money for feasibility studies into modernising and expanding their operations.

image.altText: Man in hard hats and high-ves vests stand on an industrial site.

items.title: These cranes are battery-powered as firms increasingly ditch diesel

children.content: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

image.altText: Crane in the centre of a large construction site. City buildings in the background.

items.title: Mystery streak of light from China gives Australians spectacular show

children.content: Some skywatchers describe a silvery or blue-tinted ball of gas that gradually turned red and disappeared.

image.altText: Bright silver burst in sky

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

articles.title: Somali referee returns home after being denied entry to US for World Cup

children.content: Omar Artan thanks FIFA for its support after he is barred from entering the United States, ending his dream of refereeing at his first World Cup.

image.altText: Referee Omar Artan gestures during the FIFA U-20 World Cup in 2025.

articles.title: Socceroos are a long way from home but have a special Oakland connection

children.content: Socceroos captain Mat Ryan is a long way from home, but he has found an unexpected connection in Oakland.

image.altText: Jesus De Vincente of the Oakland Roots Soccer Club and Mat Ryan of the Socceroos pose for a photo

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

image.altText: Generic 2026 FIFA World Cup football

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man lifts a golden trophy among celebrating teammates with fireworks exploding in the background.

items.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's

children.content: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

image.altText: Serena Williams celebrates a point with Victoria Mboko at Queen's.

items.title: Gary Ablett Jr joins dad in Australian Football Hall of Fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.
